pattern: Counting occurrences over 1..n reveals the one value seen twice and the one value seen zero times, returned as the duplicate then the missing.
hints: The array should be a permutation of 1..n; the error swaps exactly one value for a copy of another.
Count how many times each value in 1..n appears; one count is 2 and one count is 0.
Tally counts for 1..n. The value with count 2 is the duplicate, the value with count 0 is the missing one; return [duplicate, missing].

edge_inputs.description: duplicate in the middle, missing at the end
missing is the largest value n

stress.description: construct a valid 1..n permutation then replace one value with a copy of another
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randrange(2, max(3, min(size, 5000)))
    nums = list(range(1, n + 1))
    missing = rng.randrange(1, n + 1)
    dup = rng.randrange(1, n + 1)
    while dup == missing:
        dup = rng.randrange(1, n + 1)
    nums[missing - 1] = dup
    rng.shuffle(nums)
    return [nums]
stress.note: guarantees exactly one duplicate and one missing value
